differentials.name: Closed-lip schizencephaly
differentials.raw: Closed-lip schizencephaly (type 1) = gray matter-lined lips in contact with each other (may be missed in imaging planes parallel to the plane of cleft)

differentials.name: blindness possible (optic nerve hypoplasia in 33%) Location:   most commonly near pre- and postcentral gyri (sylvian fissure); uni- / (mostly) bilateral; in middle cerebral artery distribution
differentials.raw: blindness possible (optic nerve hypoplasia in 33%) Location:   most commonly near pre- and postcentral gyri (sylvian fissure); uni- / (mostly) bilateral; in middle cerebral artery distribution

context: =  AGENETIC PORENCEPHALY = TRUE PORENCEPHALY =  "split brain" =  rare CNS malformation consisting of a full-thickness CSF-filled parenchymal cleft lined by gray matter extending from subarachnoid space to subependyma of lateral ventricles Frequency:   1÷1,650 Cause:   ? vascular ischemia of portion of germinal matrix / genetic mutation → segmental developmental failure of neuronal cell migration to form cerebral cortex; NOT porencephaly Time of injury:   30-60 days of gestation Often associated with:   polymicrogyria, microcephaly, gray matter heterotopia, septooptic dysplasia Types: √  walls
